data.epi_content:
After pouring out all the words that had been bottled up inside me for so long, I felt exhausted—or perhaps I was just lightheaded from losing too much blood.

Vivian brought another white cloth and tied it tightly over the wound I had inflicted on myself.

What could she be thinking right now?

Originally, I shouldn't have been someone who occupied such a significant place in Vivian's life.

Is she thinking I'm just a nuisance?

I tried once more to struggle free from the bed.

No matter how hard I try to escape and end my life, there's ultimately no way out.

Biting off your tongue to die is practically impossible.

Even if I cut it off, what good would it do? Vivian would rush over and press another cloth against the severed part, and in the meantime, Evan would arrive to heal me.

There's no way out.

Yes, no way out.

I cannot escape.

My body always remains the same, but my mind is growing sick.

Yes, that's right.

I am a sick person.

I am a spiteful person.

I am a person who cannot win others' affection.

Was it said that humans are born inherently good but corrupted by their environment?

What an unpleasant notion.

Therefore, such worthless moralizing propaganda disguised as fiction should rightfully be burned.

Ah, at least there are no such writings here, which I consider truly fortunate.

Humans cannot possibly be rational beings.

If I were rational, I wouldn't have displayed such shameful behavior in front of Vivian.

I would have hidden my unstable mental state as much as possible and somehow dealt with it myself when alone in my room.

I wouldn't have provoked Lydia and slapped her, knowing full well that the intensity of the bullying would only increase.

I was in so much pain—yes, at first I was in so much pain from losing love that I clumsily tried to consume various poisons to end my life.

And to deny this, I would have to assume that I'm not human.

Perhaps I'm not—one could argue I'm livestock, since I don't even possess the freedom to die that everyone else has.

A strange version of myself has formed in my head.

A self that knows everything about me, this world, and the people around me.

I first shed tears over being a worthless human, then became mentally ill over the fact that Evan had no interest in me, and upon realizing the world was trying to bury me, I gave everything to it and shut myself away.

Occasionally, when I tried to deny my existence, that other self would poke its head out and resist, but I didn't like that, so I would cut my arms, spew curses at myself, and remove flesh and blood, removing parts of myself in the process.

In the novel, I'm supposedly the villain.

Just because I handled goods and created wealth, they apply the standards of another world to me and call me evil.

In times when people eat people, if I insist that people shouldn't eat people, I would be considered abnormal there.

But in this world where Blacks aren't considered human, it's normal not to treat them as people.

But to the one looking down from above—yes, the one and only God that Vivian spoke of—I must certainly be evil.

Well, if there are even half-Black people—which I find appalling, though it seems impossible—who are friendly with Vivian and even belong to the knighthood, then I suppose that makes me the villain.

I was living, clinging to life because I was afraid of dying, and I chose death because I was afraid of losing love.

Yet here we all are, having lost what we wanted right before our eyes, drifting around like garbage floating in the world.

In this prison of time.

In novels I've read before, people trapped in time like this would use it as an opportunity to train and become stronger.

Why can't I do the same?

Why? Because my body is so weak and frail that physical training would be meaningless.

The only weapon I have is a pistol, and even that doesn't work against people who use magic or even cold weapons.

The only ones I can kill are incompetent people like Lydia, filled with bad blood, malice, baseness, and ambition.

Even if I kill them, it means nothing.

Just like my existence means nothing.

So just let me die.

Why is this happening?
data.epi_content2:
Perhaps some disgustingly talented spellcaster cast what they called a blessing on me.

"Erica, Erica."

Someone is calling me.

Shaking my body to wake me up makes me nauseous.

"Come to your senses."

"...Evan?"

When I opened my eyes, Evan was right in front of me.

Vivian stood behind him with a worried expression, looking me over.

"Why are you here?"

"Vivian called me."

"Of course she did."

"I won't ask why you're like this."

"Why not?"

"This morning, I heard bits and pieces about what happened."

"......"

If you know, there's no need to talk about it.

Don't try to comfort me.

Don't pretend to understand.

"What, did you think dying would end everything?"

Evan asks me accusingly, as if he's angry.

Frowning with an expression identical to Vivian's, like he might cry.

Since there was a time when I thought dying would end everything, my answer came a bit late.

"...No."

I slightly open and close my mouth.

As if I might speak but then don't, I move my lips slightly before finally closing my mouth.

How could I say that I didn't cut my arm because I wanted to die, but just wanted to teach this lady a lesson because I didn't like her mocking me?

If he asks about the pistol, well...

Anyone would see me as someone who just tried to kill herself, so I can't say anything.

"Lately, even though we've grown a bit distant, we're friends, aren't we?

You said you didn't like me. Yes, I had that ominous feeling then, I guess that's why I felt strange."

Then.

If you think of me as a friend.

"Then why didn't you care about me?

Why did you leave me alone, and even push me away..."

I lowered my head and whispered in a very small voice.

"No. Nothing."

It's so easy to love everyone, especially to express love to someone of the opposite sex you're attracted to.

But it's difficult to offer a drop of hope to something familiar that's withering from thirst beside you, or to help them give up.

Because it's familiar, you think it's okay to act this way.

"Evan, what do you mean by feeling?"

"When I came looking for you yesterday, I suddenly felt extremely complicated and a bit confused.

I don't know why, but I also felt sad. It all resolved after a night's sleep though."

"...Really?"
data.epi_content3:
Perhaps Evan's emotions from experiencing slight sadness after my death also came back in time.

It sounds absurd, but when I die, time immediately rewinds and I come back to life.

"Let's talk about this over tea, now that Erica is awake."

"Alright."

Vivian says, changing the atmosphere.

I nodded, and Evan answered.

Vivian dragged a table all the way to the bed where I was lying, placed a large bowl in the center, and poured in what looked like freshly baked cookies.

I had wondered why the room was filled with the smell of baking snacks—Vivian wasn't using a brazier or anything like that, she was just baking cookies with magic.

"Coffee is ready too."

"Pour two cups of coffee, Vivian."

Vivian poured coffee into two mugs.

Then Evan created ice with a crackling sound out of thin air for the remaining cup, making iced coffee.

He handed me the cold coffee.

"What's wrong? You like it with ice."

"Did I? Yes, I did."

I take the cup and drink coffee for the first time in a while.

Both Evan and Vivian seemed very cautious about saying anything to me.

Except for the sound of sipping drinks and the crunching of cookies, no other sound could be heard in the room.

Evan was just downing his coffee while deeply contemplating something, and Vivian stared at him for a long time, then interlaced her fingers before trying to pick at a hangnail.

She must have pulled it wrong, as the skin peeled off sideways from her finger.

Vivian roughly wiped the bleeding part next to her nail, blew on it, and then acted as if nothing had happened.

Then, she casually threw a question at me.

"Hey Erica, wouldn't it be better if you stayed in my room for a while?"

"Why do you think that?"

"I can't leave you alone. I'm worried."

"Vivian, you know I don't like you very much.

And I have no intention of staying with you..."

"If you won't stay with Vivian, I'll drag you to my room instead."

Evan interrupts as I was about to refuse.

"Be reasonable."

"Why is that reasonable when your family falling to ruin overnight isn't?"

"Why are you bringing up family matters?"

"Just, Erica. Look at yourself. Just look at yourself."

Hearing those words, I looked in the mirror.

I look fine.

There's nothing strange about me.

Except for looking a bit haggard and having some blood stains on my uniform.

"I can't leave you alone. Just, well.

This isn't me asking for your permission, it's already decided.

Either you'll be in my room all day, or you'll be with Vivian all day."

"Well, both options are terrible."

After hearing Evan's words, I felt I needed to escape from here somehow.

So, as always, without thinking of the consequences, I started something emotional that had no chance of success.

I got up, feeling slightly dizzy, knocked over the table, and ran to the front door like a cat dashing away.

And of course, I was caught, feeling despair as I realized the door was locked.
